note: Onboarding-cost reduction (design: docs/novakai/onboard-cost-design.md). SCOPE: .mjs TOOLING — check coherence with --map docs/novakai/_tooling.mmd; real verification is node --test on quiz/edit-gate/onboard suites. Handoff rotation landed separately (db168ac).

intent.problem: The pass artifact is checkout-scoped: any agent (or subagent) passing the quiz unlocks src/ edits for every other agent in the checkout — the gate attests understanding the editing agent may not have. Demonstrated live 2026-07-03 by a rejected onboarder-subagent proposal that would have satisfied the gate without the orchestrator reading the map.
intent.approach: quiz check records session (--session flag, else CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID env — verified equal to the PreToolUse payload session_id this session — else null). quiz verify gains --session; when the flag is present, an artifact whose session differs (other id, null, or pre-v2) fails with a re-take reason. Flag-only activation keeps manual CLI and existing tests hash-only and environment-independent. edit-gate passes --session from payload.session_id (already in the payload, currently telemetry-only).
intent.rationale: compliance becomes agent-scoped, matching what the vision already claims it is; no existing deny path is removed. Accepted consequence: sessions stop inheriting passes — made affordable by the two scoped changes below.

intent.problem: The pass binds to the sha256 of the whole bundle, so one signature change anywhere voids the proof of every module — the honest response is a ~500k-token full re-onboard per code-landing session.
intent.approach: quiz check records per-fragment sha256 keyed by each fragment's %% root module id (all 42+1 fragments; 46/47 src .ts files resolve to exactly one module via %% src, main.ts via colocated basename). quiz verify --file requires: module within proven scope, module fragment hash current, all direct edge-neighbour fragment hashes current (adjacency from bundle edges, both directions, A5-backed). Whole-bundle hash mismatch alone no longer denies --file verifies; flagless verify unchanged. Unmappable file or missing fragment hash denies (fail closed). edit-gate passes --file and names the stale modules and scoped re-take command in the deny reason.
intent.rationale: staleness becomes local to the blast radius the map itself proves, so the gate claim sharpens (you read the current map of what you are editing and of what it touches) while the recurring cost drops to a scoped re-read.

intent.problem: Onboarding has one track: read the whole bundle (~48k tokens) and take the whole-map quiz, even for a continuity session whose blast radius is already declared by the in-flight plan.
intent.approach: quiz generate/check gain --scope <modules> (questions drawn only from those modules; check records the scope; a scoped pass unlocks only its scope). onboard gains --continue [--plan]: same STEP 1 trust proof, then root.mmd + invariants + handoff 0-now + the plan targets' fragments + scoped quiz commands, and prints verbatim the out-of-scope design-question rule; the same rule lands in the CLAUDE.md session protocol (F1). Full track byte-unchanged.
intent.rationale: continuity sessions pay for the modules they will touch (~20-30k tokens) instead of the whole app; design sessions keep the full read, which is where whole-app context genuinely prevents bad choices.
